hindiTranslation: अभ्याससे शास्त्रज्ञान श्रेष्ठ है, शास्त्रज्ञानसे ध्यान श्रेष्ठ है और ध्यानसे भी सब कर्मोंके फलका त्याग श्रेष्ठ है ।  कर्मफलत्यागसे तत्काल ही परमशान्ति प्राप्त हो जाती है ॥
englishTranslation: Knowledge is better than practice; meditation is better than knowledge. Renouncing the fruits of action is better than meditation, and peace follows at once from renunciation.
essence: Peace begins when you stop clinging to what your action produces.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Practice, study, and meditation all help, but letting go of personal reward is even higher. That release brings immediate calm.
simpleInsight: You do not have to earn peace by forcing control; it comes when grasping ends.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen between duty and grief. Krishna narrows the path step by step: practice, then understanding, then meditation, and finally the deepest release — giving up the fruits of action. That is where peace begins.
modernRelevance: You send the hard message, then keep checking your phone for the reaction. The mind burns in that waiting. This verse points to the relief of doing what is right and leaving the outcome alone.
translationLiteral: श्रेयो (better) / हि (indeed) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / अभ्यासात् (than practice) / ज्ञानात् (than knowledge) / ध्यानम् (meditation) / विशिष्यते (is superior) / ध्यानात् (than meditation) / कर्मफलत्यागः (renunciation of the fruits of action) / त्यागात् (from renunciation) / शान्तिः (peace) / अनन्तरम् (immediately after)
insight: It shifts the mind from managing outcomes to releasing them after sincere action.

quoteVariants.text: Knowing is good. Letting go of results is better, and peace comes first.
The result can be dropped. Peace arrives immediately.
Drop the result. Keep the peace.
